THE WORLD BANK

IMPLEMENTATION STATUS AND RESULTS REPORT (ISR)
Fragility-Sensitive Community Development Project

Report Date: March 15, 2026
Project ID: P123456
Country: Testland
Practice: Social Protection and Jobs
TTL: Michael D. Brown

================================================================================

PROJECT STATUS

Development Objective (DO) Rating:           Moderately Unsatisfactory
Implementation Progress (IP) Rating:         Moderately Satisfactory
Previous DO Rating:                          Moderately Satisfactory
Previous IP Rating:                          Satisfactory

DO Rating Change:                            Downgraded this period
Reason for DO downgrade:                     Escalating conflict in Northern District has disrupted
                                             targeting operations; PDO indicator 2 (targeting fairness)
                                             is below target trajectory.

================================================================================

KEY ISSUES

1. Security Situation — Northern District

Active conflict between government forces and a non-state armed group in Kodou and Maraba sub-districts
(Northern District) has forced a temporary suspension of cash transfer activities in those areas since
January 2026. Approximately 18,000 beneficiary households (7.2% of total enrolled) are currently not
receiving payments. The CERC component has been partially activated to provide emergency support to
newly displaced households in adjacent areas.

The PIU FCV Specialist has flagged elevated inter-communal tensions in Eastern District as a potential
spillover risk. Conflict dynamics are being monitored weekly; the next conflict sensitivity assessment
is scheduled for April 2026.

2. Targeting Grievances — Eastern District

A pattern of targeting-related grievances has been identified in three sub-districts of Eastern District,
primarily involving exclusion of returnee households. The GRM received 847 targeting-related complaints
in Q4 2025, up from 312 in Q3 2025. Of these, 691 have been resolved; 156 are pending investigation.
Root cause analysis indicates a systematic error in the community targeting database that excluded
households that returned after the registration cutoff date.

Corrective action: PIU is conducting a supplementary targeting exercise for returnee households, with
completion targeted by end of April 2026.

3. Financial Management

FM arrangements are functioning. The Q3 2025 Interim Financial Report was submitted on time. The
annual audit for FY2025 is underway; preliminary findings are clean. No FM issues rated substantial
or high.

4. Procurement

All procurement under the project is on track. The FCV Specialist consultancy contract was extended
for 12 months to cover the additional workload from conflict monitoring activities.

================================================================================

RISK RATINGS

Risk Category                                      Rating        Change
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Political and Governance                           High          No change
Macroeconomic                                      Moderate      No change
Sector Strategies and Policies                     Moderate      No change
Technical Design                                   Moderate      No change
Institutional Capacity                             Substantial   No change
Fiduciary                                          Moderate      No change
Environment and Social                             Substantial   No change
Stakeholders                                       High          Upgraded from Substantial
Security / Conflict Escalation                     High          No change
OVERALL                                            High          No change

Note: Stakeholders risk upgraded due to GRM complaint surge in Eastern District and heightened
community sensitivities in the context of pre-election period.

================================================================================

ACTIONS REQUIRED

Action 1 (TTL / PIU — Due April 30, 2026):
Complete supplementary returnee targeting exercise in Eastern District and reopen case files for
the 156 pending GRM complaints. Report to World Bank task team by April 30.

Action 2 (PIU FCV Specialist — Due April 15, 2026):
Conduct updated conflict sensitivity assessment for Northern and Eastern Districts. Share findings
with task team for discussion at May implementation support mission.

Action 3 (PIU / World Bank — Due March 31, 2026):
Confirm CERC activation procedures for Northern District displacement caseload and agree on
targeting parameters for emergency support packages.

Action 4 (Government / PIU — Due June 30, 2026):
Prepare midterm review preparation plan, including terms of reference for the independent
impact evaluation data collection round.

================================================================================

DISBURSEMENTS

Total Commitment:                            USD 50,000,000
Total Disbursed to Date:                     USD 31,240,000 (62.5%)
Disbursements This Period (Q4 2025):         USD 4,820,000
Disbursement Rate vs. Plan:                  94% (slightly below plan due to Northern District suspension)

================================================================================

[END OF ISR]
